hypothesis: If XGBoost models are trained to predict the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT (PBE/HSE06) band gaps using the top 400 features from the ST-135, RACs, and APRDF descriptor sets, then they will outperform direct XGBoost models predicting DFT band gaps and achieve accuracy comparable to fine-tuned MOFTransformer and CGCNN models because ∆-learning corrects systematic errors in the semi-empirical GFN1-xTB baseline.
